key_strengths: World's most-flown small-lift rocket outside SpaceX: 75+ Electron missions, 21 launches in 2025 at 100% mission success rate
Vertically integrated space company with launch, satellite manufacturing, and components under one roof — rare competitive position
Strong backlog visibility: $1.85B backlog (+73% YoY), ~3x annual revenue, with multi-year SDA government contracts anchoring Space Systems
Record FY2025 revenue of $602M (+38% YoY) with GAAP gross margin expanding to 38% in Q4 2025
Founder-led with candid operator: Peter Beck discloses failures plainly and communicates engineering reality without spin
key_risks: Neutron program failure or continued delay: already slipped to Q4 2026 after stage-1 tank test failure; 30-40% probability of further setback with catastrophic valuation impact
Multiple compression risk: trading at 66x trailing revenue (top 3-4% most expensive in global A&D); any sentiment shift or guidance miss could reset share price to $22-25
Government contract concentration: SDA Tranche 3 ($816M) represents 44% of backlog; any scope cut or cancellation would materially impair forward revenue
SpaceX structural competitive pressure: Transporter rideshare at $5,500/kg vs. Electron's ~$25,000/kg permanently limits commercial market; Falcon 9 dominates medium-lift that Neutron targets
Capital markets dependency: burns $300-400M/year in FCF with no self-funding capability; survival through 2029 depends on continued access to equity and debt markets
red_flags: ROIC deeply negative (-30%) across entire public operating history — no demonstrated capital efficiency
FY2025 FCF of -$321M is an acceleration from -$103M in FY2024, not improvement
SBC running ~30% of trailing revenue — among the highest in aerospace sector, ongoing shareholder dilution
CEO Peter Beck sold hundreds of millions in stock throughout 2025 at elevated prices, reducing direct ownership
Neutron development cost overrun: original $250-300M budget exceeded at ~$360M cumulative through 2025 with first launch still a year away
SDA single-customer concentration: one program represents ~44% of $1.85B backlog
Q4 2025 raised $280.6M via ATM equity offering — further diluting shareholders to fund operations
buy_triggers: Stock declines to $25-35 range, where bear-case intrinsic value begins to offer protection and risk/reward inverts to ~2-3x upside vs. 30-40% downside
Neutron successfully completes first orbital flight, establishing reliability track record and de-risking the primary thesis before valuation fully re-rates
GAAP gross margin reaches and sustains 40%+ for two consecutive quarters while quarterly FCF burn narrows toward $50M, demonstrating core business approaching self-funding
sell_triggers: Neutron suffers first- or second-flight failure extending commercial timeline by two or more years, eliminating medium-lift market entry and triggering cash-burn crisis
SDA Tranche 3 contract cancelled, significantly reduced in scope, or delayed more than 18 months due to government budget pressure
RKLB reaches $120-140/share without achieving GAAP profitability — valuation premium becomes indefensible on any fundamental framework
valuation_summary: At $69.70/share (~$40B market cap, 66x trailing revenue), today's price requires Neutron to successfully launch, ramp to commercial cadence, and drive revenue to $10B+ over 15 years at 15%+ net margins — a conjunction of five demanding assumptions that must all hold simultaneously. Bear-case intrinsic value of $20-35/share implies 50-70% downside, while realistic bull-case upside is only 30-43%, creating a 2:1 to 3:1 downside-to-upside ratio that is the inverse of sound investment logic.
source_summary: Analysis based on web search results (Feb-Mar 2026), Rocket Lab Q4 2025 earnings release (Feb 26, 2026), Q3 2025 earnings release, SEC filings, MacroTrends/GuruFocus/StockAnalysis financial data, and insider transaction disclosures; no user-provided context files were present.
